---
title: "Helen Webley-Brown"
image: "website-pic.jpg"
links:
  - label: Resume
    url: "CV_HWebleyBrown.pdf"
  - label: Email
    url: "mailto:helenwb@mit.edu"
  #- label: SoundCloud
  #  url: "https://soundcloud.com/"
  #- label: Twitter
  #  url: "https://twitter.com/"
output:
  postcards::trestles
---

## Hello!

I am a second-year Ph.D. student in Political Science at MIT with specializations in American Politics and Quantitative Methods.

My current research agenda fits into two broad buckets:

1.  Socio-political effects of interacting with the [criminal legal system.]{style="color:teal"}
2.  How [emerging technologies]{style="color:teal"} change the way people experience and perceive the state.

At the intersection of this, I am interested in pre-trial electronic monitoring, and the role of tools like ShotSpotter and Flock in policing. 

I also have a nascent interest in how political violence targeted at elected officials impacts the representation of gender and racial minorities.

Although my research focuses on the US, I was born and raised in London, England. I made my way across the pond via the US-UK Fulbright Commission's wonderful [*Sutton Trust US Programme*](https://us.suttontrust.com/){style="color:lightseagreen"}.

## Education

[**Massachusetts Institute of Technology**]{style="color:teal"} \| Cambridge, MA

Ph.D. in Political Science \| 2022 - Present

[**Washington University**]{style="color:teal"} \| St. Louis, MO

A.B. in Political Science \| 2018 - 2022

## Research

Gupta, A., Wu, V., **Webley-Brown, H.**, King, J., Ho, D. (2023). [*The Privacy-Bias Tradeoff: Data Minimization and Racial Disparity Assessments in U.S. Government*](https://www.dropbox.com/s/57tndd2dzg919zb/Gupta_Privacy.pdf?dl=0){style="color:lightseagreen"}. ACM Conference on Fairness, Accountability, and Transparency (FAccT).
